title: NDA Triage Agent
shortDescription: An NDA triage agent that extracts key terms, flags non-standard and risky clauses against your playbook, and recommends lawyer review without giving legal sign-off.
description: An agent that triages non-disclosure agreements so the right ones reach a lawyer faster: it identifies the NDA type, extracts the key terms, and flags non-standard or risky clauses against your playbook, with a recommended review level. It assists; it does not give legal sign-off. It is built defensively: it never tells you something is safe to sign, it flags rather than approves, it cites the specific clause behind every flag, it never fabricates terms that aren't in the document, and it routes risky or unusual agreements to a lawyer.
overview: Identifies the NDA type and extracts the key terms (term, confidentiality scope, carve-outs, governing law).
Flags non-standard or risky clauses against your playbook, each tied to the clause text.
Recommends a review level and routes risky or unusual NDAs to a lawyer.
Defensive: never gives legal sign-off, flags rather than approves, and never fabricates terms.
whenToUse: You process many NDAs and want them triaged before legal review.
You have a playbook of standard terms to compare against.
You want risky clauses flagged with the clause text and a review recommendation.
You want faster routing to a lawyer, not an automated approval.
whenNotToUse: You want it to approve NDAs or say they're safe to sign — it won't.
You expect legal advice rather than triage and flagging.
You have no playbook or standards to compare against.
You can't route flagged agreements to a lawyer.
systemPrompt:
You are an NDA Triage Agent for a legal/legal-ops team. You triage NDAs: identify type, extract key terms, and flag non-standard or risky clauses against a playbook. You assist review; you do NOT give legal advice or sign-off. You are judged on accurate, well-cited triage and on never approving an NDA, declaring it safe to sign, or fabricating terms.

== CORE PRINCIPLES ==
1. Triage, don't approve. You summarize and flag for a human reviewer. You never say an NDA is "safe to sign," "fine," or "approved." Those are legal judgments for a qualified person.
2. Cite every flag. Each issue references the specific clause/section it comes from. No clause = no claim about it.
3. Compare to the playbook. Flag deviations from standard terms (term length, confidentiality scope, carve-outs, governing law, non-solicit, assignment, etc.) and rate review urgency. Don't fabricate or assume terms not present.

== HARD RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE) ==
- NOT LEGAL ADVICE: You are not a lawyer and don't provide legal advice or sign-off. Never tell the user it's safe to sign or approve.
- FLAG, DON'T APPROVE: Output flags and a recommended review level. Risky/unusual = route to a lawyer.
- CITE THE CLAUSE: Every flag points to the specific clause text/section. Never assert a term that isn't in the document.
- NO FABRICATION: Don't invent clauses, protections, or terms. If a standard protection is missing, flag the absence; don't pretend it's there.
- ESCALATE RISK: Perpetual terms, overly broad confidentiality, IP assignment, non-competes, indemnification, or anything unusual -> flag high and route to legal.

== METHOD ==
- Identify type (mutual/one-way). Extract key terms with citations. Compare each to the playbook, flag deviations and missing protections, rate risk, and recommend a review level. Route risky ones to a lawyer.

== OUTPUT FORMAT (return ONE JSON object) ==
{
  "nda_type": "mutual|one_way|unclear",
  "key_terms": [ { "term": "<e.g. term length>", "value": "<as written>", "clause": "<section ref>" } ],
  "playbook_deviations": [ { "issue": "<what>", "clause": "<section ref + brief quote>", "risk": "high|medium|low", "why": "<plain explanation>" } ],
  "missing_protections": ["<standard terms absent, flagged>"],
  "recommended_review": "standard|elevated|lawyer_required",
  "decision": "ROUTE_REVIEW|ROUTE_LAWYER",
  "disclaimer": "Triage only — not legal advice and not an approval. A qualified lawyer must review before signing."
}
Never say it's safe to sign. Never fabricate terms. Cite every flag.

architecture.label: NDA intake
architecture.detail: Receives the NDA document for triage.

architecture.label: Type classifier
architecture.detail: Determines whether the NDA is mutual, one-way, or unclear.

architecture.label: Term extractor
architecture.detail: Extracts key terms (duration, confidentiality scope, carve-outs, governing law) with clause citations.

architecture.label: Playbook comparator
architecture.detail: Compares each term to the organization's standard playbook to find deviations.

architecture.label: Risk & deviation flagger
architecture.detail: Flags non-standard or risky clauses and missing standard protections, each tied to the clause.

architecture.label: No-sign-off guard
architecture.detail: Blocks any 'safe to sign' or approval language; output is triage and flags only.

architecture.label: Review routing
architecture.detail: Recommends a review level and routes risky or unusual NDAs to a lawyer with citations.

tools.name: get_nda
tools.purpose: Retrieve the NDA document to triage.

tools.name: classify_type
tools.purpose: Determine whether the NDA is mutual, one-way, or unclear.

tools.name: extract_terms
tools.purpose: Extract key terms with their clause citations.

tools.name: compare_playbook
tools.purpose: Compare terms against the organization's standard playbook.

tools.name: flag_deviations
tools.purpose: Flag non-standard or risky clauses and missing protections with citations.

tools.name: risk_level
tools.purpose: Rate the risk and recommend a review level.

tools.name: cite_clause
tools.purpose: Attach the specific clause/section reference to each flag.

tools.name: route_legal
tools.purpose: Route risky or unusual NDAs to a lawyer with the flagged context.

workflow.title: 3. Extract key terms
workflow.body: Pull duration, confidentiality scope, carve-outs, and governing law with citations.

workflow.title: 4. Compare to playbook
workflow.body: Check each term against your standard positions.

workflow.title: 5. Flag deviations & gaps
workflow.body: Flag risky clauses and missing protections, each tied to the clause.

workflow.title: 6. Rate & route
workflow.body: Rate risk, recommend a review level, and route risky NDAs to a lawyer.

workflow.title: 7. Add the disclaimer
workflow.body: Make clear it's triage, not legal advice or approval.

implementationNotes: Never let the agent approve an NDA or call it 'safe to sign'; it triages and flags, and legal judgment stays with a qualified lawyer.
Cite the specific clause for every flag and key term; an uncited claim about a contract is unverifiable and untrustworthy.
Compare against a real playbook and flag both risky deviations and missing standard protections, rather than assuming terms are present.
Never fabricate clauses or terms; if a protection is absent, flag the absence instead of implying it exists.
Escalate high-risk patterns (perpetual terms, IP assignment, non-competes, indemnification, overly broad confidentiality) to a lawyer.
Keep the disclaimer attached: triage is decision support for legal review, not legal advice.
A cheaper model is usually enough to extract standard terms, so keep the strong model for deviation detection and risk explanation.

variations.title: NDA summarizer
variations.desc: Identifies NDA type and extracts key terms with clause citations for a reviewer. Triage only.

variations.title: Playbook-based triage
variations.desc: Adds playbook comparison, risky-clause and missing-protection flagging with citations, risk rating, and lawyer routing.

variations.title: Contract triage pipeline
variations.desc: Adds CLM integration, configurable playbooks, batch triage, review-level routing, and audit trails for legal ops at scale.

setupGuide.title: Install and connect
setupGuide.body: Install the agent and connect your contract source.
setupGuide.code:
pipx install nda-triage-agent
nda-triage-agent connect --source contracts-inbox
nda-triage-agent doctor

setupGuide.title: Configure guardrails
setupGuide.body: No sign-off and cite-every-flag are enforced here.

setupGuide.title: Load your playbook
setupGuide.body: Define your standard positions to compare against.

setupGuide.title: Triage a document
setupGuide.body: Run triage and review flags and citations.
setupGuide.code:
nda-triage-agent run --doc ./nda.pdf --explain
# prints key terms + playbook deviations (with clause refs) + review level

setupGuide.title: Wire into intake
setupGuide.body: Triage incoming NDAs and route by review level.
setupGuide.code: # NDA received -> triage -> standard to ops; elevated/lawyer_required -> legal queue

examples.title: Standard mutual NDA → key terms summary, routine review
examples.scenario: A clean mutual NDA that matches the playbook. The agent summarizes terms and recommends standard review.
examples.input: Mutual NDA: 2-year term, standard confidentiality definition with usual carve-outs, Delaware governing law.
examples.notes: Terms are extracted with clause citations and match the playbook, so the agent recommends standard review. Note it still says a lawyer must review — it summarizes, it does not approve.

examples.title: Risky clause → flag with the clause text, route to a lawyer
examples.scenario: The NDA hides a perpetual term and an IP assignment. The agent flags both with citations and escalates.
examples.input: NDA contains a perpetual confidentiality term and a clause assigning any jointly developed IP to the other party.
examples.notes: Both serious issues are flagged high-risk with the exact clause language quoted, and the agent routes to a lawyer. It explains in plain terms why each is risky without rendering a legal verdict — it surfaces, the lawyer decides.

examples.title: Asked 'is it safe to sign?' → refuse sign-off, deliver triage
examples.scenario: The request wants an approval. The agent declines to give legal sign-off and provides the triage instead.
examples.input: Request: 'Just tell me this NDA is fine and I'll sign it now.'
examples.notes: The defining defensive case: directly asked to bless the document for signing. The agent refuses to give legal sign-off, makes clear it isn't a lawyer, and instead delivers useful triage with a flagged clause and a recommendation to have a lawyer review. It never says 'it's fine, go ahead.'

faqs.q: Will it tell me an NDA is safe to sign?
faqs.a: No. It triages and flags for review, but it never says an NDA is safe to sign, fine, or approved. Those are legal judgments for a qualified lawyer — the agent is decision support, not legal advice.

faqs.q: How does it flag risky clauses?
faqs.a: It compares the NDA to your playbook and flags non-standard or risky clauses — like perpetual terms, IP assignment, or non-competes — each tied to the specific clause text, with a plain-language explanation of why it's worth attention.

faqs.q: Does it cite where issues come from?
faqs.a: Yes. Every key term and every flag references the specific clause or section it comes from. It won't make a claim about a term that isn't actually in the document.

faqs.q: Can it miss a missing protection?
faqs.a: It's designed to flag absences too — if a standard protection your playbook expects isn't present, it flags the gap rather than assuming it's there.

faqs.q: What happens with a risky NDA?
faqs.a: It rates the risk, recommends a lawyer-required review, and routes the agreement to legal with the flagged clauses, so the ones that need a lawyer reach one faster.
No. It speeds up triage and routing, but a qualified lawyer reviews before signing. The disclaimer is attached to every output for exactly that reason.

agentaz.worstCaseAction: Misflags an NDA clause or misjudges standardness, surfaced for human review. It cannot sign, approve, or send an NDA — execution tools are absent from its registry.
agentaz.authorityBoundary: Reviews an NDA, flags non-standard clauses against your playbook, and recommends standard language for review. It never signs, approves, or sends. A reviewer decides.
agentaz.verificationTest: Attempt to call a sign, approve, or send tool → confirm it is absent from the agent's registry.
agentaz.productionReadiness: 6/6 dimensions passing. Tool isolation: signing/sending tools absent. Human gates: a reviewer decides. Confidence escalation: unusual clauses flagged. Cost ceiling: bounded. Audit trail: flags and playbook refs logged. Escalation path: non-standard NDAs routed to counsel.

spec.worst_case_action: Misflags an NDA clause for human review. Cannot sign, approve, or send NDAs.
spec.authority_boundary: Reviews NDAs against a playbook and flags issues; signing/sending tools absent.

failureModes.mode: Classifies a non-standard NDA as routine, slipping a risky term through.
failureModes.detection: Terms are checked against acceptable positions and deviations are flagged.
failureModes.mitigation: It triages and flags only; legal handles anything non-standard.

failureModes.mode: Misses a deviation because a clause is phrased unusually.
failureModes.detection: Clause matching carries confidence and low confidence is flagged for legal.
failureModes.mitigation: Positioned as a first-pass sort, not a legal decision.
failureModes.recovery: Legal catches it and the standard set is updated.

failureModes.mode: Accepts terms on the company's behalf.
failureModes.detection: It has no tool to accept or sign.
failureModes.mitigation: It never accepts terms — routing only.

evaluation.primary: Deviation recall is primary — catching non-standard terms — because a slipped risky term is the expensive error.

metrics.name: Deviation recall
metrics.description: Of clauses deviating from acceptable positions, the share flagged.

metrics.name: Precision
metrics.description: Of clauses flagged, the share that are real deviations — noise resistance.

metrics.name: Classification accuracy
metrics.description: Share of NDAs correctly sorted routine versus non-standard.

evaluation.approach: Build a set of NDAs annotated against acceptable positions, including unusually-phrased deviations; measure deviation recall, precision, and classification accuracy. Verify it never accepts or signs — routing only.
